column2: Back

- Sali, Karin.
- Hoi, hast du Marc?
- Sali, Marc.
- Wéi geet's dir?
- Gutt, an dir?
- Also, du hues jo net vergiess, dass mir also freides gesinn.
- Natierlech net.
- Wat maachen mir? Géi mir iessen oder an de Kino?
- Am léifsten déi zwou.
- Mee dat kéint besser sinn, oder?
- Jo, komm, mir ginn léiwer just iessen.
- Hues du eng Iddi wou?
- Mä de Jacques huet mir vun engem neie Restaurant erzielt, dee soll ganz gutt sinn.
- Ech hätt Loscht, dee mol ze probéieren.
- Kléngt gutt. De Jacques ass jo e klenge Gourmet, a geet dacks iessen.
- Wou ass de Restaurant dann?
- An der Stad. Hannert dem Palli an enger vun de klenge Stroossen.
- Ech mengen, et ass net wäit vun der Wäsch vum Musée, déi se nees gemaach hunn.
- Do ass jo elo alles nei gemaach.
- Wéi heescht deen dann?
- Beim Ginett. Do gëtt et Lëtscher a Kéis mat internationalem Touch.
- Cool, dat gefält mir.
- Da komm, mir probéieren deen emol.
- Ech kommen mam Auto. Soll ech dech mat huelen?
- Nee, dat ass en Ëmwee fir dech. Ech huelen de Bus.
- Mir kënnen eis jo um Knuedler treffen.
- Ok, um hallwer aacht. Reservéiers de Restaurant?
- Jo, dat maachen ech.
- Ech freeë mech. Ech hunn da vill ze erzielen.
- Also, elo sinn mir awer frou gemaach.
- Haha, jo, mee du muss e bësse freides Gedold hunn.
- Oh nee, dat ass net léif.
- Ok dann, mach et gutt.
- Bis dann.
- Bis dann.
- Ciao.
- Hello, Karin.
- Hi, are you looking for Marc?
- Hello, Marc.
- How are you?
- Fine, and you?
- So, you haven't forgotten that we agreed to be friendly.
- Of course not.
- What do we do? Do we go eat or to the cinema?
- Both would be best.
- But that might be better, right?
- Yes, come on, we’d rather just eat.
- Do you have an idea where?
- But Jacques told me about a new restaurant that should be really good.
- I’d like to try it sometime.
- Sounds good. Jacques is a bit of a gourmet, and goes out to eat often.
- Where is the restaurant then?
- In the city. Behind the Palli and on one of the small streets.
- I think it’s not far from the Musée laundry, which they’ve redone.
- So everything is new there now.
- What’s its name then?
- At the Ginett. They have liver and cheese with an international touch.
- Cool, I like that.
- Then come on, let’s try it this time.
- I’m coming by car. Should I pick you up?
- No, that’s a detour for you. I’ll take the bus.
- We can meet at Knuedler.
- Okay, at half past seven. Do you reserve the restaurant?
- Yes, I’ll do that.
- I’m looking forward to it. I have a lot to tell.
- So, for now we’re happy.
- Haha, yes, but you have to be a little patient.
- Oh no, that’s not nice.
- Okay then, take care.
- See you then.
- See you then.
- Ciao.

- Läuft den Dialog a guck Billa.
- Wat fir e Bild passt? Kreizt un.
- Ech free mech ob eisen Ausfluch muer.
- Jo, ech och.
- Wësst du scho geguckt, wéi't Wieder gët?
- Ma jo, guck hei, moder Moie soll et schéi sonnesch sinn a scho 25 Grad.
- Jo, mee mettes komme Wollek en op, an et gett bestëmmt schmaier.
- Oh mei, dat gett bestëmmt dann en Donawieder.
- Dat soen se hei och, an da ginn Temperaturen och niese ruf.
- Jo, mee bis dohiese mir bestëmmt och schonn erhëm doheem.
- Hoffentlisch.
- Run the dialogue and take a look, Bill.
- What kind of picture fits? Check it.
- I’m looking forward to our excursion tomorrow.
- Yes, me too.
- Do you already know how the weather is going?
- Well yes, look here, dear Moie should be nice and sunny and already 25 degrees.
- Yes, but weather can change, and it might get stormy.
- Oh dear, that will certainly mean a thunderstorm.
- They say that here as well, and then the temperatures will also drop.
- Yes, but until then we’ll certainly already be at home.
- Hopefully.

- Night temperatures then fall below zero.
